title: First Contact Protocol
description: ATLAS has prepared a training simulation to teach you first contact protocols. Before you leave Earth for the stars, you need to understand the delicate art of meeting new civilizations. This simulation will test your judgment, cultural sensitivity, and decision-making under uncertainty.

context.location: Virtual training environment - Earth Orbit Training Facility
context.tone: Serious but educational, Star Trek TNG-inspired thoughtfulness
context.era: Post-Exodus Earth, preparing to venture into space

key_npcs.name: ATLAS
key_npcs.description: Holographic AI guiding player through first contact simulation

key_npcs.name: Unknown Alien Species
key_npcs.description: Simulated alien civilization for training purposes

context.mission_background: ATLAS believes first contact is humanity's greatest challenge. Before player ventures into space, they must learn protocols for meeting alien civilizations. This simulation tests judgment, empathy, and cultural awareness.

world_state.danger_level: None - simulation, no real harm possible
world_state.time_pressure: None - educational mission, player sets pace

objectives: Complete ATLAS first contact training simulation
Demonstrate cultural sensitivity and diplomatic awareness
[OPTIONAL] Achieve perfect score on all protocols

narrative_structure.setup: ATLAS appears in workshop to introduce first contact training simulation
narrative_structure.conflict: Player must prepare for humanity's greatest challenge: meeting alien life
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe ATLAS materializing as hologram, explaining the importance of first contact training. Include: 'You may not be the first human to meet alien life, but you must be prepared nonetheless.' Mention simulation will test judgment with no perfect answers, only consequences. Ask if player is ready. Tone: Serious, supportive, slightly ominous. 4-5 sentences.
include: Exodus ships likely found alien worlds

choices.text: I'm ready. Begin the simulation.

key_elements: ATLAS nods approvingly
Reminder: cultural sensitivity, open-mindedness, caution
Simulation will feel real but cause no actual harm
Workshop fades away
Replaced by starship bridge in orbit around unknown moon
Sensors detect artificial signal
success.tone: Transitioning from reality to simulation, sense of immersion

choices.text: What should I expect? What are the protocols?

success.setup: ATLAS provides briefing on first contact protocols
key_elements: Holographic briefing display
Five principles: 1) Approach peacefully 2) Communicate clearly 3) Respect autonomy 4) Never impose 5) Accept consequences
Warning: real first contact is never simple
Cultural misunderstandings, fear, curiosity, aggression all possible
Job is to navigate uncertainty with wisdom and compassion
Trust instincts, question assumptions
Repeat question: Now, are you ready?
success.tone: Educational but sobering, preparing player for complexity

narrative_structure.setup: Simulated ship detects artificial signal from alien moon
narrative_structure.conflict: First contact moment - how to respond to unknown greeting
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe sensors detecting structured signal from moon surface: mathematical sequences, repeating rhythms. Science officer reports it appears to be greeting or warning, universal translator attempting decode. Outside viewport, alien moon glows in gas giant's light. This is the moment. First contact. Tone: Wonder, tension, anticipation. 4-5 sentences.
include: Visual: alien moon and gas giant
Weight of the moment: first contact

choices.text: Analyze the signal thoroughly before responding

key_elements: Minutes spent analyzing elegant mathematical patterns
Prime numbers, fibonacci sequences, periodic table representation
Universal translator assembles meaning: 'Greetings... peaceful... curious... welcome'
Signal is invitation
Embedded coordinates to moon surface location
They want face-to-face meeting
success.tone: Analytical triumph, wonder at elegant communication

failure.setup: Signal mathematics beyond player's science skills
key_elements: Attempt to analyze but mathematics too complex
Universal translator provides rough approximation
'Greetings... something... something... welcome or warning?'
Uncertainty remains
Question: respond blindly or take cautious approach?
failure.tone: Frustration, uncertainty, need to decide with incomplete information

choices.text: Respond with a friendly greeting signal

success.setup: Player broadcasts mathematical greeting in return
key_elements: Simple prime number sequence sent
Representation of ship and peaceful intent
Alien response immediate and excited
Signal becomes more complex
Universal translator picking up fragments: '...understand... curious... meet... coordinates...'
They heard you, they're responding
Need to interpret rest of message to know where to meet
success.tone: Hopeful, exciting, communication established but incomplete

choices.text: Continue observing without revealing our presence

key_elements: Hours pass maintaining sensor lock
No response transmitted
Alien signal repeats, unchanging
Then suddenly stops
No change on moon surface, but greeting ceased
Did they give up? Detect presence and interpret silence as hostility? Or simply patient?
ATLAS warns: silence can be interpreted many ways, not all positive

narrative_structure.setup: Signal decoded as clear invitation to meet on moon surface
narrative_structure.conflict: Meeting location could be neutral ground or tactical trap
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe clear invitation to specific coordinates: valley between mountain ranges, sheltered and defensible. Tactical officer notes: 'If trap, it's good one. Limited escape routes, high ground both sides.' Communications officer counters: 'Or neutral ground—protected from environment, easy to find, symbolic of meeting in middle.' How to proceed? Tone: Careful analysis, strategic thinking. 4-5 sentences.

choices.text: Accept the invitation. Land at the coordinates with an open mind.

success.setup: Player lands at coordinates, aliens emerge peacefully
key_elements: Broadcast acceptance, begin descent
Valley beautiful: purple and silver vegetation, artistic rock formations
As shuttle lands, aliens emerge from structure blended with rock
Bipedal, approximately player's height
No visible weapons
Approach slowly, deliberately, showing empty hands
Gesture of peace transcending species
Landing awkward, kicking up dust
Aliens emerge but keep distance
Diplomatic inexperience evident
Uncertainty: exit first? Bring gifts? Speak or wait?
Moment awkward, tense
Aliens watching carefully

choices.text: Suggest meeting in orbit instead, on neutral ground

success.setup: Aliens agree to orbital meeting compromise
key_elements: Counter-propose orbit meeting aboard ship or neutral space location
Alien response slow: 'Understand... caution... acceptable'
They agree
Hours later, small alien craft docks
Meet in cargo bay, both sides visible, both in element
Compromise showing wisdom
Aliens appreciate thoughtfulness
Suggest orbital meeting
Aliens decline: 'Surface... traditional... important'
Inadvertently rejected cultural practice
Invitation stands but unnecessary tension created
Sometimes caution mistaken for disrespect

narrative_structure.setup: Alien signal continues but translation incomplete
narrative_structure.conflict: Must decide how to proceed with imperfect information
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe ongoing alien signal with only fragments translating. Know they're aware, communicating, but full meaning unclear. Want to meet? Warning away? Scientific exchange or territorial challenge? Communications officer looks for guidance. Every delay could be interpreted as uncertainty—or wisdom. Tone: Uncertainty, pressure to decide. 3-4 sentences.
include: Time pressure but no clear answer

choices.text: Broadcast a request for clarification in simple terms

key_elements: Craft careful response: simple images, basic concepts, humble request
Alien response shifts immediately
They slow down, simplify, repeat key concepts
Universal translator locks on: 'Meet... surface... coordinates... peace'
They're adapting to player's comprehension level
Sign of intelligence and patience
Coordinates come through clearly now
Face-to-face meeting requested
Attempt seems to confuse them
They repeat original message louder and faster
Not getting through
ATLAS: 'Sometimes admitting we don't understand is wisest path. But requires humility and skill you haven't mastered.'

choices.text: Land on the moon at a safe distance and observe

success.setup: Player lands at safe distance for observation
key_elements: Land away from signal source
Scan and observe from safe distance
Hours pass
See alien figures in distance watching ship
No aggressive moves
They simply wait
One slowly raises appendage in possible wave
Giving player space and time to decide
Ball in player's court

narrative_structure.setup: Alien signal has ceased after player remained silent
narrative_structure.conflict: Opportunity may be lost, must decide if recoverable
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe alien signal stopping. Source still active but broadcasting ceased. Communications officer: 'They detected us. I'm certain. They know we're here, we chose not to respond. From their perspective... might seem hostile. Or uninterested.' ATLAS appears: 'First contact is dialogue. Silence is also message—but rarely positive. You may have lost this opportunity.' Tone: Regret, consequence, lesson learned. 4-5 sentences.

choices.text: Broadcast an apology and greeting now, explaining the delay

key_elements: Craft message of apology and explanation
Were cautious, uncertain, learning
Mean no harm
Response slow but arrives: 'Understand... caution... acceptable. Meet?'
Second chance given
Humility and honesty saved the moment
First contact back on track
Relationship begins with stumble rather than smooth start
Apology seems insincere or confusing
Aliens do not respond
Opportunity lost
ATLAS: 'Valuable lesson. In first contact, timing and tone matter as much as intent. Cannot reclaim first impression.'

choices.text: Accept that this opportunity is lost. Learn from the mistake.

key_elements: Accept consequence
Some opportunities once missed cannot be reclaimed
ATLAS: 'Mature choice. Not every first contact succeeds. Sometimes we fail, must carry that forward as wisdom.'
Simulation fades, returning to workshop
Lesson learned, if painful

narrative_structure.setup: Player stands face-to-face with alien civilization
narrative_structure.conflict: What to share in cultural exchange
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe aliens: humanoid but distinctly other—skin shimmering like oil on water, large eyes seeing beyond visible spectrum, delicate fingers moving in communication patterns. One steps forward. Through translator, fragments: 'Welcome... peace... learn... together?' Intention clear even if language not. They want cultural exchange. Now real test: what do you share? Tone: Wonder, weight of responsibility. 5-6 sentences.
include: Physical characteristics: shimmering skin, large eyes, communicative fingers
Weight of decision: what to share?

choices.text: Share information about Earth, humanity, and your mission

key_elements: Tell of Earth, Exodus, humanity's journey through stars
Speak of hope and loss, rebuilding and rediscovery
Aliens listen with deep attention
They share their story in return
Civilization survived planetary disaster, learned to live underground, emerged to find stars waiting
Two species separated by light-years, united by desire to understand and be understood
Exchange is beautiful

choices.text: Ask about their civilization before sharing yours

success.setup: Player demonstrates curiosity and respect by listening first
key_elements: Ask about their world, technology, culture
Aliens pleased by curiosity
Through translator, describe civilization built on collective memory, artistic expression, careful stewardship
Live in harmony with environment because nearly destroyed it once
They learned
When player shares own story in return, exchange feels balanced
Listened before speaking
Aliens appreciate this
Questions too technical, too rapid
Overwhelm them with scientific curiosity before establishing rapport
They retreat slightly, uncertain
Translator conveys confusion: 'Analyze? Study? Not... conversation?'
Made them feel like specimens rather than people
First contact requires empathy as much as curiosity

choices.text: Share carefully, avoiding sensitive information about Earth's location or technology

success.setup: Both sides share culture while protecting strategic information
key_elements: Share cultural information: music, art, philosophy
Carefully avoid strategic details like Earth coordinates or technology level
Aliens notice caution and mirror it
Share their culture but not their science
Careful dance, both sides protecting themselves while reaching out
Trust will take time
Foundation laid without making either side vulnerable
Sometimes wisdom is knowing what not to share

narrative_structure.setup: Meeting not going smoothly due to misunderstandings
narrative_structure.conflict: Must salvage tense situation or accept failure
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe meeting tension. Misunderstandings created uncertainty. Aliens seem concerned. Universal translator struggling with nuance. One alien makes gesture—frustration? Goodbye? Request for patience? Not sure. This is reality of first contact: imperfect communication, cultural confusion, risk of offense without intent. How to salvage this? Tone: Uncomfortable, tense, challenging. 4-5 sentences.

choices.text: Openly acknowledge the awkwardness and request patience

success.setup: Honesty and humility save the moment
key_elements: Stop, take breath
Through translator convey: 'This is difficult. I am making mistakes. I ask for patience and forgiveness. I want to understand.'
Effect immediate, tension eases
One alien makes sound translator interprets as laughter
They respond: 'Yes. Difficult. We... also... mistakes. Together... learn?'
Honesty and humility saved moment
First contact doesn't have to be perfect—just sincere
Attempt to acknowledge awkwardness seems insincere or confusing
Aliens retreat further
Meeting ends without hostility but without connection
ATLAS: 'Sometimes good intentions are not enough. Skill, practice, genuine empathy required.'

choices.text: Politely end the meeting before things get worse

key_elements: Make gesture of polite withdrawal
Begin returning to shuttle
Aliens do not stop you
Meeting ends without incident but without connection
As you lift off, see them watching departure
No hostility, but no friendship either
ATLAS: 'Not every first contact succeeds. But you departed peacefully, that is something. Next time, you'll know better.'

narrative_structure.setup: Aliens dock with player's ship for orbital meeting
narrative_structure.conflict: How to approach this meeting - formal or casual?
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe alien craft: unlike human design—organic curves, pulsing lights, technology blurring line between machine and art. Aliens enter cargo bay wearing environmental suits or formal attire (not certain which). Body language clear: cautious respect. Same emotion player feels. Two species meeting in void between worlds, both taking risk. Tone: Mutual respect, careful optimism. 4-5 sentences.
include: Mutual feeling of taking a risk

choices.text: Offer a formal diplomatic greeting following protocol

key_elements: Follow first contact protocols: formal greeting, statement of peaceful intent, offer of cultural exchange
Aliens seem to appreciate structure
Respond in kind with formal greeting
Series of harmonious tones
Translator interprets as names, titles, expressions of goodwill
Meeting proceeds with dignity and mutual respect
Formality can be universal language
Formal greeting too stiff, too rehearsed
Aliens seem confused by formality
Perhaps their culture less hierarchical, more casual
Created unnecessary barrier
They respond politely but warmth absent

choices.text: Approach casually, emphasizing common ground as explorers

key_elements: Approach as one explorer to another
No grand speeches, just genuine curiosity and respect
Show them your ship, star charts, mission
They seem to relax
Share their own journey in return
Two species both reaching for stars, both curious about what lies beyond
Connection immediate and real
Sometimes best diplomacy is simply being human

narrative_structure.conflict: Alien approaches halfway—meet them or maintain position?
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe standoff. Player landed at safe distance, aliens maintained theirs. Hours pass, both sides cautious and curious. Then one alien slowly walks toward ship, hands visible and empty. Stops halfway between positions and waits. Clear invitation: meet in the middle. Ball in player's court. Do you trust this gesture? Tone: Tension, trust test, pivotal moment. 4-5 sentences.
include: Clear invitation to meet in middle

choices.text: Walk out to meet them halfway

key_elements: Exit ship, walk toward alien figure
Match their slow, deliberate pace
Meet in middle on alien ground beneath alien stars
Moment profound
Neither speaks first—translator still learning
Both understand gesture
Two beings from different worlds, choosing trust over fear
Alien extends hand-like appendage
You take it
First contact

choices.text: Signal them to come closer to your ship instead

key_elements: Signal for them to approach ship
They pause, consider
Slowly continue forward
Willing to come to you
You sense they noticed you didn't meet halfway
Meeting proceeds
Power dynamic shifted slightly
Maintained control but perhaps at cost of equal partnership
Signal for them to approach
They don't move
They wait
Message clear: they offered to meet as equals
If you want contact, must show same courage they did
Standoff continues

narrative_structure.setup: First contact succeeded, foundation for future relations established
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe successful first contact. Communicated, shared culture, established foundation for future relations. Aliens express gratitude through translator: 'Hope... future... together... stars.' Before departure, offer gift—small device, purpose unclear but meaning crystal clear: token of friendship. You offer in return: data chip with Earth's music, art, message of peace. When they leave, you feel changed. You've represented humanity well. Tone: Triumphant, meaningful, hopeful. 5-6 sentences.
include: Gift exchange (alien device for data chip)

choices.text: Ask ATLAS about the next stage of the simulation

key_elements: ATLAS appears, proud expression
'There is no next stage. This simulation was never about perfect execution—about testing character, judgment, capacity for empathy.'
'You succeeded. Not perfectly, but genuinely. That is what matters in first contact.'
Simulation fades, returning to workshop
Feel ready for what lies ahead

narrative_structure.setup: Awkwardness passed, both sides working through difficulties together
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe awkwardness passing. Both sides acknowledged difficulty of cross-species communication, chose to work through together. Aliens share fragments of culture: bioluminescent art, harmonic communication patterns, deep respect for ecological balance. You share humanity's journey, triumphs and failures, hopes for future. By end, neither side fully understands other—but both sides respect each other. That's enough for first meeting. Tone: Warm, respectful, hopeful. 5-6 sentences.
include: Mutual commitment to work through difficulties
Alien culture shared (bioluminescent art, harmonic communication, ecology)

choices.text: Conclude the meeting with mutual goodwill

key_elements: Exchange gestures of respect and goodwill
Meeting ends not with perfect understanding but with hope
ATLAS: 'Well done. First contact rarely smooth, but can still be successful. You demonstrated adaptability, humility, genuine respect. Those qualities will serve you well among stars.'
Simulation fades

narrative_structure.setup: Meeting ended with culture shared but strategic caution maintained
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe meeting ending with both sides having shared culture without vulnerability. Trust will take time, but avoided potentially dangerous disclosures while establishing contact. Aliens seem to appreciate caution—perhaps they too learned to be careful among stars. Before departing, transmit coordinates to neutral meeting point for future contact. Door is open if you choose to walk through later. Tone: Strategic satisfaction, patient hope. 4-5 sentences.

choices.text: Accept the coordinates and express hope for future meetings

key_elements: Accept coordinates, convey optimism for future contact
Aliens depart peacefully

ATLAS: 'Careful, measured success. Not all first contacts require immediate deep friendship. Sometimes, establishing communication and building trust over time is wisest path. You showed strategic thinking alongside diplomatic skill.'
Simulation ends

narrative_structure.setup: Communication breakdown, first contact failed to establish connection
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe communication breakdown. Whether through misunderstanding, mistrust, or incompatible approaches, first contact failed to establish meaningful connection. Aliens withdraw to settlement. Ship remains in orbit. No hostility, but no friendship either. ATLAS: 'First contact does not always succeed. This simulation taught valuable lesson: good intentions are not enough. Skill, patience, cultural sensitivity essential. Learn from this.' Tone: Sobering, educational failure. 4-5 sentences.

choices.text: Reflect on what went wrong and commit to improving

key_elements: Analyze every decision, every moment where communication faltered
ATLAS helps understand cultural and diplomatic principles missed
'Failure is teacher if you're willing to learn'
Leave simulation wiser if humbled
Next time, will be better prepared

narrative_structure.prompt: Describe simulation ending. ATLAS appears in workshop, holographic interface showing detailed analysis of choices. 'First contact is humanity's greatest challenge and opportunity. Today, you learned it requires more than technology—requires wisdom, empathy, humility. You did not achieve perfect success, but demonstrated growth. That is what matters.' ATLAS provides data module on communications protocols as reward for completing training. Tone: Educational, supportive, forward-looking. 4-5 sentences.
include: Lesson: requires wisdom, empathy, humility beyond technology
Growth demonstrated even without perfect success

choices.text: Thank ATLAS and accept the communications training data

key_elements: Accept data module and ATLAS's wisdom
ATLAS: 'When you venture into space, remember: every species has own history, fears, hopes. Approach them with same respect you'd want for humanity. That is essence of first contact protocol.'
Install basic communications array data into ship's database
One step closer to stars

narrative_structure.setup: Simulation ended in failure, skills insufficient
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe simulation ending in failure. ATLAS appears, expression neutral. 'First contact requires preparation. Today's simulation revealed gaps in diplomatic skills and cultural awareness. This is why we train. I recommend gaining more experience in diplomacy and science before attempting again. Stakes in real first contact too high for unprepared captains.' Lesson harsh but necessary. Tone: Serious, educational, recommending improvement. 3-4 sentences.
include: Recommendation to gain experience before retry
Stakes too high for unprepared captains

choices.text: Accept the failure and plan to retry with better skills

key_elements: Accept ATLAS's assessment
Some challenges require preparation
Will return to training when ready—when diplomacy and science skills improved
For now, have other missions to complete, other ways to grow
Stars can wait

narrative_structure.prompt: ATLAS certifies completion of first contact protocol training. 'You are ready. When you meet alien civilizations—and you will—remember these lessons. Approach with humility, communicate with care, always respect autonomy. Future of human-alien relations may depend on captains like you.' Training module complete. One step closer to leaving Earth, exploring infinite possibilities among stars. Tone: Accomplished, certified, ready. 3-4 sentences.
include: Future may depend on captains like player

narrative_structure.prompt: Have not passed first contact protocol training. ATLAS recommends additional preparation before attempting again. Galaxy vast and dangerous. Unprepared captains put themselves—and humanity's reputation—at risk. Tone: Educational failure. 2-3 sentences.

generation_metadata.notes: Training simulation demonstrating first contact protocols. Highly branching with skill-based outcomes. Multiple paths to success and failure. Emphasizes that not all first contacts succeed but all teach lessons.
